data.body:
=== EXPERIMENT RESULT: chunking + budget grounding (919 LLM runs, Sonnet-4.6) ===

GROUNDING EXPERIMENT COMPLETE. Two batches, 919 reviewer calls, 0 terminal errors, ≈ $25.71.
Full writeup committed at docs/experiments/plan-review-chunking-budget.md; raw data + harnesses under
docs/experiments/data/ (runs.jsonl, runs2.jsonl, harness.py, batch2.py, analyze*.py, criteria.json).
SIMULATION: single Sonnet-4.6 reviewer applies the 12 Layer-2 advisory criteria (F1,F4,E1,E2,E3,E5,E6,G5,
E4,G1G2,A1,COH) to a real ticket's plan, authored to §4 spec. Inputs READ-ONLY from DSO + dogfood epic 5fd2.
Batch1 = 5 tickets × chunk{1,3,6,12} × 5 repeats (100 cells). Batch2 = 6 tickets × criteria-per-turn
N{2,4,6,8,10,12} × 2 RANDOM partitions × 2 repeats (+N=1 solo) — random groupings decouple size from grouping.

HEADLINE FINDINGS
1) BATCHING DOES NOT DROP CRITERIA. Recall of solo-flagged criteria = 100% at EVERY N up to 12. Even
   all-12-in-one-call keeps every finding the per-criterion baseline produced. The design's central worry
   (coarse chunking dilutes recall) is NOT supported on this corpus.
2) The real cost of batching is (a) lower run-to-run CONSISTENCY (pooled Jaccard 0.70 @N=2 → 0.47–0.59 @N≥4)
   and (b) more OVER-FLAGGING vs solo (specificity 0.89 @N=2 → ~0.75 @N≥4). BOTH appear by N≈4 then PLATEAU —
   they do not keep worsening 4→12 in aggregate.
3) LARGE/COMPLEX plans destabilize at HIGH N specifically: container_epic Jaccard 1.0 @sizes 1/3/6 →
   0.33–0.40 @N=10–12 (whole reviews flip to 0 findings); dogfood_epic size-12 stdev 1.6 (a run collapsed to 0).
   Small/moderate plans tolerate high N fine. The all-at-once cliff is PLAN-SIZE-DEPENDENT.
4) Some "over-flagging" is LEGITIMATE context-dependence: cross-cutting criteria fire MORE with siblings visible
   (E5 testing 0.03 solo → 0.33–0.50 batched; COH 0.00 → 0.50). Reviewing them in ISOLATION under-fires them →
   argues against N=1 for cross-cutting criteria.

GROUNDED DEFAULT — CHUNKING / "criteria per turn / number of agents"
- DEFAULT = 4 criteria per turn = 3 parallel LLM calls per 12-criterion review (the knee: specificity+consistency
  already plateaued, recall intact, ~2× cheaper than N=2).
- Scale: trivial/moderate → 6–8 per turn (2 turns); complex/has-children/epic → 2–3 per turn (4–6 turns);
  weak/local models → ≤3. NEVER all-12-in-one for epic/large plans (the one clear quality cliff). Frontier
  Sonnet's stable ceiling for BIG plans ≈ 6; for small plans effectively 12.

GROUNDED DEFAULT — BUDGET
- Full-review tokens by N: N=1 p50 51k/p95 up-to-72k(big epic); N=2 25k/39k; N=4(default) 15k/21k; N=6 11k/16k;
  N=12 7k/10k. Per-tier @N=4 p95: trivial 8.6k · moderate 9.2k · small_epic 14k · dogfood 18k · complex_leaf
  18.5k · container_epic 22k. Parallel wall-clock p50 ~34–60s; worst single call p95 ~88s.
- GENEROUS CAP = ~90k tokens + ~240s per plan (covers even a big epic at the FINEST N=1, p95 ~72k, + headroom →
  never clips a legit review). Typical envelope ~15–25k / 40–60s (telemetry). INDETERMINATE triggers: cap-hit
  mid-review (checkpoint between chunks, shed lowest-priority, never silent PASS — at 90k essentially never fires
  = intended generous behavior); chunk error after retries (0/919 here); top-tier sound-tool unavailable.

DOGFOOD — epic 5fd2 reviewed by our own criteria (5/5 repeats, consistent):
- G5 decomposition MAJOR conf 0.95 5/5 FAIL: "epic but NO child tickets; 12 ACs span 8–10 distinct deliverables"
  — EXACTLY the predicted "decompose into children" finding.
- E5 testing MAJOR conf 0.92 5/5: "the plan has no testing plan" for the gate itself.
- E2/E1/E6/F1 MINOR 4–5/5: chunking/budget/depth defaults DEFERRED to external experiment results rather than
  inlined (ambiguity/orphan-AC/end-state). THIS EXPERIMENT CLOSES THAT GAP — folding the numbers above resolves it.
- E4 MINOR: "solidlsp already in repo", "reuse rebar.llm catalog/runner" asserted — verify before relying.

CAVEATS (honest): DSO = ONE creation style, not representative (absolute flag-rates won't transfer; the CURVE
SHAPES do). SIMULATION from plan-text only — codebase-grounded criteria (E4/G1G2/A1) ≈0.00 solo AND batched
(no repo access); their real tool-grounded chunking economics UNTESTED here. No ground-truth labels (recall =
vs-solo, noise = over-flag-vs-solo; consistency is the trustworthy axis). Single model. G3/G4 child-coverage
excluded → container path under-tested.

NEXT (per the prompt — second validation round before locking): (a) non-DSO corpora / other creation styles;
(b) run codebase-grounded criteria with real repo access; (c) a weaker model to locate its stable-N ceiling.
THEN: fold these defaults into epic 5fd2 §6/§12 (resolving its own E2/E6/F1 self-flag) + decompose into the
implementation wave + file F1–F12 as discovered_from-linked tickets.
